content.problemStatement:
We define f(A)f(A)f(A) as the minimum positive integer XXX such that the following condition holds:


For each iii (1ŌēżiŌēżŌłŻAŌłŻ1 \le i \le |A|1ŌēżiŌēżŌłŻAŌłŻ), there exists jŌēĀij \ne ijŅĆĀ=i (1ŌēżjŌēżŌłŻAŌłŻ1 \le j \le |A|1ŌēżjŌēżŌłŻAŌłŻ), such that ŌłŻAiŌłÆAjŌłŻŌēżX|A_i - A_j| \le XŌłŻAiŌĆŗŌłÆAjŌĆŗŌłŻŌēżX.
For arrays of size 111, i.e. ŌłŻAŌłŻ=1|A| = 1ŌłŻAŌłŻ=1, we define f(A)=0f(A) = 0f(A)=0. For other arrays, it can be proven there exists at least one such integer.

You are given an integer NNN. Let B=[1,2,3,ŌĆ”,N]B = [1, 2, 3, \ldots, N]B=[1,2,3,ŌĆ”,N].

Consider the 2NŌłÆ12^N - 12NŌłÆ1 non-empty subsequences CCC of the array BBB possible, find the sum of f(C)f(C)f(C) over all such CCC.

Since the answer may be large, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output the sum of f(C)f(C)f(C) over all subsequences of [1,2,ŌĆ”,N][1, 2, \ldots, N][1,2,ŌĆ”,N].

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are 333 subsequences, [1][1][1], [2][2][2], and [1,2][1, 2][1,2]. The third one has f(C)=1f(C) = 1f(C)=1, the others have f(C)=0f(C) = 0f(C)=0.
Test Case 2 : There are 777 subsequences, [1][1][1], [2][2][2] and [3][3][3] have f(C)=0f(C) = 0f(C)=0. [1,2][1, 2][1,2], [2,3][2, 3][2,3] and [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] have f(C)=1f(C) = 1f(C)=1. Finally, [1,3][1, 3][1,3] has f(C)=2f(C) = 2f(C)=2. The sum is 555.
